message:
test(ftui): add unit test matrix for state reducer, compat layer, and views (FTUI-07.1)

State reducer (29 new tests, 55 total):
- PrevView, CycleProfile, ClearError
- Events filter family: Push/Pop/Clear/ToggleUnhandled
- History filter family: Push/Pop/Clear/ToggleUndoable
- CycleSavedSearchNext/Prev with wrapping and empty cases
- SelectPrev per-view dispatch, Triage/History/Search selection
- SelectNext noop for Home/Help, DataRefreshed preserving triage_expanded

ftui_compat (13 new tests, 23 total):
- Area: zero width, split_top_zero, non-empty
- StyleSpec: reversed builder, default all false, bg-only
- ColorSpec: RGB equality
- KeyInput: new no modifiers, non-char key, all modifiers, F-key
- InputEvent: Resize fields
- ScreenMode: InlineAuto

ftui_stub fixes:
- Fixed LavenderGrove's TextInput.set_text() cursor placement (end vs 0)
- Adapted History tests to TextInput API (filter_query -> filter_input)
- Fixed MockQuery::with_search_results() builder calls
- Fixed modal test Frame::new pool parameter and zero-height guard

3058 library tests pass, 0 failures. Both --features ftui and tui compile clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-1pe.5","title":"Dry-run tests: verify no state modifications, output correctness, error paths","description":"
# Dry-Run Testing Suite

## Purpose
Ensure dry-run mode never modifies state and produces accurate predictions.

## Test Categories

### 1. No State Modification Tests
- Dry-run send: DB unchanged, no WezTerm CLI calls
- Dry-run workflow: No locks acquired, no step logs
- Dry-run approve: Approval state unchanged

### 2. Output Correctness Tests
- Target resolution matches actual resolution
- Policy evaluation matches actual evaluation
- Step plan matches actual execution order

### 3. Error Path Tests
- Invalid pane: helpful error in dry-run output
- Policy denial: shows what would block
- Expired approval: shows expiry clearly

### 4. Format Tests
- TTY output is human-readable
- JSON output is valid and complete
- All fields present in both formats

### 5. Contract Tests
- Dry-run output fields match execution fields
- No drift between preview and reality

## Testing Approach
- Mock WezTerm CLI to detect any calls
- Compare dry-run output to actual execution logs
- Snapshot tests for output stability

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 100% coverage of dry-run code paths
- [ ] Verified no state modification in any test
- [ ] Output snapshots for regression detection
- [ ] Error message quality verified

 {"id":"wa-1qr1","title":"FTUI-07.3.a Deterministic PTY fixture seed/timing strategy","description":"## Background
PTY E2E suites are only trustworthy if timing and input generation are deterministic and anti-flake by design.

## Deliverables
- deterministic seed policy for PTY scenario generation
- timing/control strategy that removes sleep-based nondeterminism
- anti-flake constraints (timeouts, retries, jitter controls, artifact requirements)

## Acceptance Criteria
- repeated runs produce stable outcomes and comparable logs across environments
- suite guidance specifies required transcript/log metadata for debugging
- strategy is adopted by FTUI PTY scenarios and referenced from CI gate bead.,","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:37.597376381Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:05.428526618Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","ftui","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1qr1","depends_on_id":"wa-3gii","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:37.616084937Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-1qr1","depends_on_id":"wa-bjvg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:25:34.127403507Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}

 {"id":"wa-1ssn","title":"[EPIC] FTUI-04 Query-to-View Adapter Layer and State Model","description":"## Purpose
Build stable, testable data/state adapters between wa query surfaces and ftui view models.

## Why
UI rewrites fail when data contracts are implicit. We need explicit typed adapters and deterministic state reduction.

## Focus
- query facade contracts
- adapter and mapping layers
- state reduction, caching, and refresh semantics
- annotation/history/workflow model integrity for operators","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:14.856391035Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:13:57.821742735Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:13:57.821673246Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data","ftui","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1ssn","depends_on_id":"wa-1k52","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:54.935848549Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-1ssn","depends_on_id":"wa-2wed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:14.878819950Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-1ssn","depends_on_id":"wa-p85q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:52.616142907Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}],"comments":[{"id":271,"issue_id":"wa-1ssn","author":"GrayHarbor","text":"### Execution Rationale (FTUI-04)
FTUI-04 isolates data contracts from rendering logic, enabling deterministic state handling and safer schema evolution during migration.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:26:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-1uqi","title":"FTUI-03.2.a Route in-process logs through single output sink","description":"## Background
In-process output must obey one-writer constraints to keep rendering deterministic and avoid output corruption.

## Deliverables
- inventory and removal plan for direct stdout/stderr writes in UI-active paths
- unified output sink routing contract for logs/events/status lines
- verification checks to prove no bypass writes remain

## Acceptance Criteria
- all targeted paths route through the sanctioned sink while UI is active
- checks detect and fail on new bypass writes
- validation evidence includes unit assertions and runtime logs demonstrating routing correctness.,","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:05.996650629Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T21:31:38.625318959Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","one-writer","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1uqi","depends_on_id":"wa-3cso","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:06.014840191Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-1uqi","depends_on_id":"wa-3g47","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:24:35.653843104Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}

 {"id":"wa-37x.3","title":"Chaos testing harness: fault injection framework for resilience validation","description":"
# Chaos Testing Harness

## Purpose
Inject failures systematically to validate wa's resilience under adverse conditions.

## Fault Injection Points
```rust
pub trait FaultInjector {
    fn maybe_fail(&self, point: FaultPoint) -> Result<()>;
}

pub enum FaultPoint {
    WezTermCliCall,
    DbWrite,
    DbRead,
    BrowserAction,
    NetworkRequest,
    FileSystemOp,
}
```

## Test Scenarios

### 1. WezTerm Unavailable
- Kill WezTerm during capture
- Verify: wa degrades gracefully, resumes when available

### 2. DB Corruption
- Corrupt SQLite file mid-write
- Verify: wa detects corruption, reports error, doesn't crash

### 3. Network Timeouts
- Inject timeout on webhook calls
- Verify: circuit breaker trips, notifications queue

### 4. Resource Exhaustion
- Fill disk to capacity
- Verify: wa stops writing, logs error, continues observing

### 5. Queue Overflow
- Fill event queue beyond capacity
- Verify: backpressure applied, no data loss

## Harness Design
```rust
pub struct ChaosHarness {
    injectors: HashMap<FaultPoint, Box<dyn FaultInjector>>,
    scenario: ChaosScenario,
}

pub struct ChaosScenario {
    faults: Vec<ScheduledFault>,
    duration: Duration,
    assertions: Vec<ChaosAssertion>,
}
```

## CI Integration
- Chaos tests run in isolated environment
- Failures generate detailed reports
- Key scenarios gated on merge

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fault injection framework implemented
- [ ] At least 5 chaos scenarios defined
- [ ] All scenarios pass (system recovers)
- [ ] Chaos tests run in CI (nightly)

## Testing
- Unit tests for breaker/backoff logic and state transitions.
- Integration tests simulating IO failures and recovery paths.
- Chaos harness scenarios with verbose logs and recovery assertions.

 {"id":"wa-37x.4","title":"Retry with exponential backoff: standardized retry policy across all I/O","description":"
# Retry with Exponential Backoff

## Purpose
Provide a consistent, well-tuned retry mechanism for all fallible operations.

## Retry Policy
```rust
pub struct RetryPolicy {
    pub initial_delay: Duration,    // 100ms default
    pub max_delay: Duration,        // 30s default
    pub backoff_factor: f64,        // 2.0 default
    pub jitter_percent: f64,        // 10% default
    pub max_attempts: Option<u32>,  // None = infinite
}

pub async fn with_retry<T>(
    policy: &RetryPolicy,
    operation: impl AsyncFn() -> Result<T>,
) -> Result<T>;
```

## Jitter Formula
```
delay = base_delay * jitter_factor
jitter_factor = 1.0 + random(-jitter_percent, +jitter_percent)
```

## Application Points
- WezTerm CLI calls: 3 attempts, 100ms initial
- DB writes: 5 attempts, 50ms initial
- Webhook delivery: 5 attempts, 1s initial
- Browser actions: 2 attempts, 500ms initial

## Logging
Each retry logged with:
- Attempt number
- Delay applied
- Error that triggered retry

## Integration with Circuit Breaker
- Retries happen within circuit breaker
- Exceeded retries count as circuit failure
- Circuit OPEN prevents retry attempts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RetryPolicy struct with configurable parameters
- [ ] with_retry helper function
- [ ] Jitter implemented correctly
- [ ] All I/O points use retry policy
- [ ] Logging shows retry attempts

## Testing
- Unit tests for breaker/backoff logic and state transitions.
- Integration tests simulating IO failures and recovery paths.
- Chaos harness scenarios with verbose logs and recovery assertions.

 {"id":"wa-37x.5","title":"Graceful degradation modes: continue operating with reduced functionality","description":"
# Graceful Degradation Modes

## Purpose
When components fail, continue operating with reduced functionality rather than crashing.

## Degradation Scenarios

### 1. DB Read-Only Mode
**Trigger**: DB write failures (corruption, disk full)
**Behavior**:
- Continue observing panes
- Queue writes in memory (bounded)
- Log warning about degraded mode
- Resume normal operation when DB recovers

### 2. Pattern Engine Failure
**Trigger**: Pattern compilation error, regex timeout
**Behavior**:
- Skip pattern detection
- Continue ingesting output
- Log which patterns are disabled
- Partial detection if some patterns work

### 3. Workflow Engine Pause
**Trigger**: Workflow step fails repeatedly
**Behavior**:
- Pause the failing workflow
- Continue other workflows
- Keep observing for new events
- Notify user of paused workflow

### 4. WezTerm Unavailable
**Trigger**: CLI consistently fails
**Behavior**:
- Stop action attempts
- Continue any cached observation
- Poll for WezTerm availability
- Resume automatically when available

## Status Reporting
```
$ wa status

System Status: DEGRADED

Active Degradations:
  ⚠ DB writes queued (disk full) - 47 pending
  ⚠ Workflow paused: handle_usage_limits (step 3 failed)

Core Functions:
  ✓ Observation: active
  ✓ Detection: active
  ✗ Actions: degraded
```

## Recovery
- Automatic: Poll for recovery conditions
- Manual: `wa recover <component>` to retry

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each degradation mode implemented
- [ ] Status reports degraded state clearly
- [ ] Automatic recovery when conditions clear
- [ ] Manual recovery command works
- [ ] Core observation continues in all modes

## Testing
- Unit tests for breaker/backoff logic and state transitions.
- Integration tests simulating IO failures and recovery paths.
- Chaos harness scenarios with verbose logs and recovery assertions.

 {"id":"wa-3akp","title":"FTUI-05.4.a Events view annotation/label/triage visibility parity checklist","description":"## Background
Events view drives operator incident response; migration must preserve annotation/label/triage visibility semantics.

## Deliverables
- parity checklist for feed ordering, annotation context, labels, triage state, and redaction-sensitive fields
- validation scenarios for high-volume event streams and mixed-severity events
- artifact mapping to snapshots, adapter fixtures, and E2E transcripts

## Acceptance Criteria
- event semantics match legacy expectations or are documented as intentional deltas
- redaction behavior is explicitly verified and logged

 {"id":"wa-3g9","title":"[EPIC] Performance Optimizations: Lazy Loading, Caching, Pooling","description":"# [EPIC] Performance Optimizations

## Mission
Make wa **fast and efficient** without sacrificing safety or correctness.

## Why This Matters
wa runs continuously in the background. Performance impacts:
- CPU usage (battery life on laptops)
- Memory footprint
- Latency of operations
- Scalability to many panes

Optimizations must be:
- Measurable (benchmarks prove value)
- Safe (no correctness regressions)
- Conditional (only apply when beneficial)

## Components

### 1. Lazy Pattern Compilation
Do not compile regex/Aho-Corasick engines until first use:
```rust
static PATTERN_ENGINE: Lazy<PatternEngine> = Lazy::new(|| {
    PatternEngine::compile(load_packs())
});
```

Benefits:
- Faster startup time
- Memory saved if patterns not used
- Hot-reload ready

### 2. Bloom Filter Pre-Filter
Before running Aho-Corasick, check Bloom filter:
```rust
if !bloom_filter.possibly_contains(&text) {
    return vec![]; // Quick reject
}
// Only then run full pattern matching
```

Budget: < 1µs for Bloom check

### 3. Connection Pooling for WezTerm CLI
Keep warm connections to WezTerm mux server:
```rust
struct WezTermPool {
    connections: Vec<MuxConnection>,
    max_size: usize,
}
```

Benefits:
- Avoid process spawn overhead
- Reduce socket churn
- Enable connection reuse

Note: Only beneficial if vendored mode or mux protocol direct access.

### 4. Memory-Efficient Output Cache
Already exists as `wa-4vx.4.12` - enhance with:
- LRU eviction by memory pressure
- Rolling hash for dedup detection
- Configurable cache size limits

### 5. Background Segment Compression
Already gated by `wa-4vx.3.11` decision - if needed:
- Compress segments older than N days
- Use zstd for good ratio and speed
- Transparent decompression on read
- Background compaction thread

### 6. Incremental FTS Sync
Ensure FTS index only processes new content:
- Track last-indexed segment per pane
- Batch index updates
- Avoid full reindex on restart

## Benchmarks
All optimizations must be validated by Criterion benchmarks:
- Baseline before optimization
- Result after optimization
- Regression tests to prevent backsliding

## Testing
- Benchmark suite for each optimization
- Memory profiling under sustained load
- No correctness regressions (same outputs)

## Success Criteria
- Startup time < 100ms (no feature flags)
- Pattern quick-reject < 1µs for non-matching text
- Memory usage < 100MB for typical workload
- CPU usage < 5% when idle

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each optimization yields measurable latency or memory improvement.
- Performance budgets are enforced in CI.
- Regression thresholds and baselines are documented.

 {"id":"wa-3g9.1","title":"Lazy pattern compilation: defer engine initialization until first use","description":"
# Lazy Pattern Compilation

## Purpose
Defer compilation of pattern matching engines until they're actually needed, improving startup time.

## Current Behavior
PatternEngine compiles all patterns at startup, even if pattern matching isn't used in this run.

## Proposed Behavior
```rust
use once_cell::sync::Lazy;

static PATTERN_ENGINE: Lazy<PatternEngine> = Lazy::new(|| {
    tracing::debug\!("Compiling pattern engine (first use)");
    PatternEngine::compile(load_packs())
});
```

## Benefits
- Faster startup for non-watch commands
- Memory saved if patterns not used
- Enables future hot-reload capability

## Benchmark Targets
- Startup time: reduce by ~50-100ms (depends on pattern count)
- First pattern match: may have one-time ~100ms delay
- Overall: net positive for typical usage

## Implementation Notes
- Use `once_cell::sync::Lazy` for thread-safe lazy init
- Ensure compile errors surface correctly
- Consider warming on `wa watch` start

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pattern engine compiled lazily
- [ ] Commands that don't use patterns are faster
- [ ] First pattern match latency acceptable
- [ ] Benchmark proves improvement

## Testing
- Criterion benchmarks with budgets and baseline comparison.
- Microbenchmarks for the specific optimization.
- CI regression guard with perf logs.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeFox","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:53:50.210760868Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:34:16.831539510Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T08:34:16.831472695Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-patterns","area-perf","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-3g9.1","depends_on_id":"wa-3g9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-dug.6","title":"Setup wizard: guided first-run configuration with detection","description":"# Setup wizard

## Purpose
Provide a guided first-run experience that uses detection to suggest optimal configuration.

## Wizard Flow

### Step 1: Welcome & Detection
```bash
wa setup

╭────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Welcome to wa - WezTerm Automata                                           │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                            │
│ Let's configure wa for your environment.                                   │
│                                                                            │
│ Detecting your setup...                                                    │
│                                                                            │
│   ✓ WezTerm 20260101 detected                                              │
│   ✓ Socket found at /run/user/1000/wezterm-mux                            │
│   ✓ Shell: zsh 5.9                                                         │
│   ⚠ OSC 133 not enabled (optional but recommended)                        │
│   ✓ 2 panes detected                                                       │
│   ✓ Codex agent detected in pane 0                                         │
│                                                                            │
│ Press Enter to continue...                                                 │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Step 2: Confirm Auto-Configuration
```bash
╭────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Recommended Configuration                                                   │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                            │
│ Based on your environment, we recommend:                                   │
│                                                                            │
│   Poll interval:    100ms (good for your system)                          │
│   Pattern packs:    core.common, core.codex                               │
│   Storage:          ~/.local/share/wa                                      │
│   Safety mode:      standard (approval for sends)                          │
│                                                                            │
│ [A]ccept recommended  [C]ustomize  [S]kip setup                           │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Step 3: Customization (if selected)
```bash
╭────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Customize Configuration                                                     │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                            │
│ Poll interval (ms) [100]: 150                                              │
│                                                                            │
│ Safety mode:                                                               │
│   (•) Standard - require approval for sends                                │
│   ( ) Permissive - auto-approve within workflows                           │
│   ( ) Strict - require approval for everything                             │
│                                                                            │
│ Enable caut integration? [Y/n]: y                                          │
│                                                                            │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Step 4: OSC 133 Setup (if needed)
```bash
╭────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Enable OSC 133 (Optional)                                                   │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                            │
│ OSC 133 helps wa detect command boundaries more accurately.                │
│                                                                            │
│ To enable for zsh, add this to ~/.zshrc:                                   │
│                                                                            │
│   # Enable semantic zones for WezTerm                                      │
│   precmd() { print -Pn "\e]133;A\a" }                                      │
│   preexec() { print -Pn "\e]133;C\a" }                                     │
│                                                                            │
│ [C]opy to clipboard  [S]kip  [?] Learn more                               │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Step 5: Complete
```bash
╭────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ Setup Complete!                                                             │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                            │
│ Configuration saved to: ~/.config/wa/wa.toml                               │
│                                                                            │
│ Next steps:                                                                │
│   • Run `wa watch` to start monitoring                                     │
│   • Run `wa status` to see current state                                   │
│   • Run `wa learn` to try the interactive tutorial                         │
│                                                                            │
│ Need help? Run `wa help` or visit https://wa.dev/docs                      │
│                                                                            │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Implementation
```rust
pub struct SetupWizard {
    env: DetectedEnvironment,
    term: Terminal,
}

impl SetupWizard {
    pub async fn run(&mut self) -> Result<Config> {
        self.show_welcome().await?;
        self.show_detection().await?;
        
        let config = match self.prompt_config_choice().await? {
            Choice::Accept => self.auto_config(),
            Choice::Customize => self.run_customization().await?,
            Choice::Skip => Config::default(),
        };
        
        if !self.env.shell.osc_133_enabled {
            self.offer_osc_133_setup().await?;
        }
        
        self.save_config(&config).await?;
        self.show_complete().await?;
        
        Ok(config)
    }
}
```

## Testing
- Unit tests for wizard state machine
- Tests for each step's rendering
- Tests for config generation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Welcome shows detection results
- [ ] Auto-config recommendations shown
- [ ] Customization flow works
- [ ] OSC 133 instructions accurate per shell
- [ ] Config file saved correctly
- [ ] Can skip wizard entirely
- [ ] Tests cover wizard flow","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T18:38:59.279802546Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:51:57.529557868Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T01:51:57.529340754Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-config","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-dug.6","depends_on_id":"wa-dug","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":247,"issue_id":"wa-dug.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GrayRidge claiming. Implementing setup wizard in setup.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:35:18Z"},{"id":248,"issue_id":"wa-dug.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented SetupWizard in setup.rs with 11 tests. Wizard uses DetectedEnvironment + AutoConfig to probe system and generate optimal wa.toml. Updated run_guided_setup in main.rs to use wizard (async). Commit a6a568b.","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:51:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-dug.7","title":"Environment detection tests: all detection types, edge cases, E2E","description":"# Environment detection tests (unit + integration + E2E)

## Purpose
Comprehensive, deterministic test coverage for the environment detection system:
- detect WezTerm presence/version/socket
- detect shell + OSC 133 integration state
- detect agent types (from pane metadata/output)
- auto-configuration mapping (detections → optimal wa config)
- E2E integration via the standard harness/runner/registry

This bead is intentionally detailed because environment detection drives first-run UX (`wa doctor`, `wa setup`, and “it just works” defaults).

## Test categories

### 1) WezTerm detection tests (unit)
Use a mock environment / command runner abstraction.
```rust
#[test]
fn detects_wezterm_version() {
    let env = MockEnv::new()
        .with_command("wezterm", "--version", "wezterm 20260101-abc123");

    let info = WeztermInfo::detect_with_env(&env);
    assert_eq!(info.version, Some("20260101-abc123".into()));
}

#[test]
fn handles_missing_wezterm() {
    let env = MockEnv::new().without_command("wezterm");

    let info = WeztermInfo::detect_with_env(&env);
    assert!(info.version.is_none());
    assert!(!info.is_running);
}
```

### 2) Shell detection tests (unit)
Detect shell type and whether OSC 133 integration appears installed.
```rust
#[test]
fn detects_bash() {
    let env = MockEnv::new().with_env("SHELL", "/bin/bash");

    let info = ShellInfo::detect_with_env(&env);
    assert_eq!(info.shell_type, ShellType::Bash);
}

#[test]
fn detects_osc_133_in_zshrc() {
    let env = MockEnv::new()
        .with_env("SHELL", "/bin/zsh")
        .with_file_content("~/.zshrc", "precmd() { print -Pn \"\\e]133;A\\a\" }");

    let info = ShellInfo::detect_with_env(&env);
    assert!(info.osc_133_enabled);
}
```

### 3) Agent detection tests (unit)
```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn detects_codex_from_title() {
    let panes = vec![mock_pane(0, "Codex - Project")];

    let agents = detect_agents(&panes).await;
    assert_eq!(agents.len(), 1);
    assert_eq!(agents[0].agent_type, AgentType::Codex);
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn no_false_positives_on_empty_pane() {
    let panes = vec![mock_pane(0, "zsh")];

    let agents = detect_agents(&panes).await;
    assert!(agents.is_empty());
}
```

### 4) Auto-configuration tests (unit/integration)
Validate the mapping layer (detections → config):
- polling interval adapts under load (bounded)
- safety strict in “production-ish” environments
- pattern packs match detected agents

Key constraint: deterministic tests (no wall-clock dependence).

### 5) E2E test (standard harness)
The E2E goal is to validate the *full integration* of detection into user surfaces, deterministically:
- `wa doctor` reports the expected environment snapshot
- auto-config output is valid and matches expectations
- `wa setup --dry-run` can consume detections and produce a stable plan

**Hard rule:** E2E must not depend on the host’s real state (no `pgrep wezterm`, no reading the user’s actual `~/.zshrc`, no ambient sockets).

#### Approach (hermetic stubbed environment)
Within the E2E harness workspace:
- Create a temp `HOME` with controlled shell rc files.
- Provide a fake `wezterm` binary in a temp dir and prepend it to `PATH`.
  - It must return deterministic outputs for `--version` and `cli list`.
- Run:
  - `wa doctor --format json`
  - `wa robot config --show-auto --format json` (or the canonical auto-config surface)
  - `wa setup --accept-defaults --dry-run`

#### Assertions
- Doctor JSON contains the expected detection fields (wezterm version, shell type, osc_133 enabled/disabled as configured by fixtures).
- Auto-config JSON contains required fields and respects detection-derived decisions.
- Setup dry-run output is deterministic and references only the E2E workspace paths.
- No secrets appear in stdout/stderr or artifacts.

#### Artifacts
- `doctor.json` (redacted)
- `auto_config.json`
- `setup_dry_run.txt`
- `env.txt` (PATH/HOME summary; redacted)
- `e2e.log` (timestamped steps + durations)

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests cover wezterm/shell/agent detection and avoid false positives.
- [ ] Auto-config tests cover all mapping rules and are deterministic.
- [ ] E2E case follows the standard harness contract and is hermetic (no host dependence).
- [ ] Artifacts/logging are detailed enough to debug failures without rerunning locally.

## Testing
- Unit: WezTerm/shell/agent detection with mock env and file fixtures.
- Integration: auto-config outputs validated for required fields.
- E2E: harness-based scenario with artifact capture (no ambient host probes).
- Determinism: all waits bounded; no fixed  {"id":"wa-dwa","title":"[EPIC] E2E Test Harness: Scripted scenarios, detailed logging, CI integration","description":"# [EPIC] E2E Test Harness

## Mission
Provide a **comprehensive E2E testing framework** with scripted scenarios, detailed logging, and CI integration that validates wa works correctly in real-world conditions.

## Why This Matters
Unit tests validate components in isolation. E2E tests validate **the whole system works together**:
- WezTerm integration actually works
- Patterns detect real agent output
- Workflows execute correctly end-to-end
- Error recovery handles real failures

Without E2E tests, we ship bugs that only manifest in production.

## E2E Test Architecture

### Test Harness
```
tests/e2e/
├── harness/
│   ├── mod.rs           # Test harness library
│   ├── fixtures.rs      # Test fixtures and setup
│   ├── assertions.rs    # Custom assertions
│   └── logging.rs       # Detailed test logging
├── scenarios/
│   ├── basic_ingest.rs  # Basic text capture
│   ├── pattern_detection.rs
│   ├── workflow_execution.rs
│   ├── policy_enforcement.rs
│   └── error_recovery.rs
├── scripts/
│   ├── run_all.sh       # Run all E2E tests
│   ├── setup_wezterm.sh # Setup test WezTerm instance
│   └── cleanup.sh       # Cleanup after tests
└── fixtures/
    ├── codex_session.txt
    ├── claude_session.txt
    └── error_scenarios/
```

### Detailed Logging
Every E2E test produces detailed logs:
```
[2026-01-18T14:30:00.123Z] [INFO] === Starting E2E Test: workflow_execution ===
[2026-01-18T14:30:00.124Z] [DEBUG] Setting up test environment...
[2026-01-18T14:30:00.125Z] [DEBUG] Starting WezTerm mock pane...
[2026-01-18T14:30:00.200Z] [INFO] Pane created: id=99, title="test-codex"
[2026-01-18T14:30:00.201Z] [DEBUG] Injecting test output: "Usage limit reached..."
[2026-01-18T14:30:00.500Z] [INFO] Event detected: codex.usage_limit_reached
[2026-01-18T14:30:00.501Z] [DEBUG] Workflow started: handle_usage_limits
[2026-01-18T14:30:01.000Z] [INFO] Step 1/7 completed: exit_codex
[2026-01-18T14:30:02.000Z] [INFO] Step 2/7 completed: wait_for_summary
...
[2026-01-18T14:30:10.000Z] [INFO] Workflow completed successfully
[2026-01-18T14:30:10.001Z] [INFO] === E2E Test PASSED: workflow_execution (9.878s) ===
```

### Test Scenarios

#### 1. Basic Ingest
- Start wa watch
- Create mock pane with text
- Verify text captured in DB
- Verify FTS indexed

#### 2. Pattern Detection
- Inject known patterns into pane
- Verify events detected
- Verify event metadata correct
- Test false positive rejection

#### 3. Workflow Execution
- Trigger workflow via event
- Verify each step executes
- Verify final state correct
- Test workflow resume after restart

#### 4. Policy Enforcement
- Attempt blocked actions
- Verify policy denies correctly
- Test approval flow
- Verify audit trail

#### 5. Error Recovery
- Simulate WezTerm disconnect
- Verify graceful degradation
- Verify reconnection works
- Test circuit breaker behavior

### CI Integration
```yaml
# .github/workflows/e2e.yml
e2e-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  services:
    wezterm:
      image: wezterm/wezterm:latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Build wa
      run: cargo build --release
    - name: Run E2E tests
      run: ./tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh
      env:
        E2E_LOG_LEVEL: debug
        E2E_TIMEOUT: 300
    - name: Upload logs
      if: always()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: e2e-logs
        path: tests/e2e/logs/
```

### Test Fixtures
Pre-recorded terminal sessions for reproducible testing:
```rust
struct TestFixture {
    name: &'static str,
    pane_title: &'static str,
    output_chunks: Vec<OutputChunk>,
    expected_events: Vec<ExpectedEvent>,
}

static CODEX_USAGE_LIMIT: TestFixture = TestFixture {
    name: "codex_usage_limit",
    pane_title: "codex @ /project",
    output_chunks: vec![
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 0, text: "Working on task..." },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 5000, text: "\n\nYou've reached your usage limit.\n" },
        OutputChunk { delay_ms: 100, text: "Daily limit resets in 4 hours.\n" },
    ],
    expected_events: vec![
        ExpectedEvent { rule_id: "codex.usage_limit_reached", .. },
    ],
};
```

## Success Criteria
- E2E tests cover all major user journeys
- Tests produce detailed, actionable logs
- CI runs E2E tests on every PR
- Test failures are reproducible locally
- Test runtime < 5 minutes for full suite

## Testing
- Harness unit tests: fixture replay ordering, log writer, and custom assertions.
- Integration tests: scenario registry + harness lifecycle (setup/teardown).
- E2E: a minimal smoke scenario that exercises the harness itself and produces an artifact bundle.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Harness can run at least the five core scenarios with deterministic ordering.
- Each scenario emits a structured log with timestamps and a PASS/FAIL summary.
- CI runs the harness and uploads artifacts for every run.
- Failures are reproducible locally using the same fixture set.

 {"id":"wa-e2jh","title":"FTUI-02.2 Define upstream sync/pinning policy for /dp/frankentui","description":"## Background\nUsing ftui across the board requires predictable intake of upstream changes.\n\n## Deliverables\n- version pin + bump cadence\n- change-review checklist for upstream updates\n- compatibility risk checklist for API drift\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- policy is documented and automated where feasible\n- upgrade path is reproducible by future maintainers.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderGrove","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:41.205715220Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:32:32.242306738Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:32:32.242240956Z","close_reason":"Documented in ADR-0009: pin cadence (weekly during migration, mandatory before release), pin procedure (6-step), change-review checklist (5 items), compatibility risk table (5 risks with mitigations), automation references.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","upstream"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-e2jh","depends_on_id":"wa-1k52","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:41.218825871Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-e2jh","depends_on_id":"wa-1utb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:15:51.003080049Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-e69a","title":"FTUI-08.3 Terminal/mux compatibility certification matrix","description":"## Background\nwa operators run diverse terminal/mux stacks; migration must be proven across them.\n\n## Deliverables\n- compatibility matrix (wezterm, tmux, screen, zellij, SSH contexts)\n- per-environment pass/fail notes and mitigations\n- known limitations documented for rollout gates\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- matrix is complete for supported environments\n- blockers are tracked with explicit mitigations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:09:35.642992803Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:23:26.622944421Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compat","ftui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-e69a","depends_on_id":"wa-1kut","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:09:36.597133597Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-e69a","depends_on_id":"wa-3gii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:23:26.622897664Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
 {"id":"wa-eutd","title":"FTUI-02.4 Add build guardrails to prevent dual-stack drift","description":"## Background\nWithout guardrails, both ratatui and ftui logic can silently diverge.\n\n## Deliverables\n- CI checks for prohibited imports in migrated modules\n- feature-matrix build checks\n- lint/docs rule for where legacy stack is still allowed\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- accidental dual-render-path changes fail fast in CI\n- allowed exception list is explicit and minimal.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderGrove","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:47.111944184Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:31:23.003168590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:31:23.003104481Z","close_reason":"Added build guardrails: (1) scripts/check_ftui_guardrails.sh with 9 checks — mutual exclusion, individual compilation, import isolation, clippy compliance. (2) ftui added to CI feature matrix. (3) ftui-guardrails CI job. (4) Exception list documented in script comments. All checks pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","guardrails"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-eutd","depends_on_id":"wa-1k52","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:47.125525960Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"},{"issue_id":"wa-eutd","depends_on_id":"wa-1utb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:16:09.182497504Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor"}]}
